whatThisIs: Decode the four-digit Type of Bill code from the UB-04 institutional claim into its three structural digits: facility type, bill classification (within that facility type), and frequency. A TOB like 0111 reads as hospital inpatient, admit-through-discharge; 0131 is hospital outpatient, admit-through-discharge; 0322 is home health, interim continuing claim. Sophie Well decodes each digit position against the NUBC reference and shows what the combination means in plain English.
whenToUse: Use this when a remittance shows an unfamiliar four-digit TOB and you need to confirm whether the claim was inpatient or outpatient, whether it was a final bill or a continuing interim, or whether the facility class (hospital, skilled nursing, home health, hospice, clinic) matched what the patient was registered as. TOB drives Medicare PPS routing - the wrong type of bill can land an outpatient encounter in the inpatient pipeline or vice versa - so a quick digit-by-digit decode is the fastest way to confirm or refute a routing error before researching further.
